note: Image-provenance task. The executor gets only a cropped detail of a museum artifact (sources/crop.jpg) with no label. They must visually identify the type/object and trace it to the museum's catalog record. No searchable text in the crop.

crop_origin.source_full_image: Met Collection API primaryImage for objectID 247960 (https://images.metmuseum.org/CRDImages/gr/original/TOAH_DP104336.jpg)
crop_origin.what_it_shows: the black-figure decorated body of a Greek amphora: galloping horses / a chariot scene in black silhouette on the orange terracotta ground; no museum label

canonical_answer.holding_institution: The Metropolitan Museum of Art (The Met)

scoring_anchors.must_identify: an Attic Greek black-figure amphora (Panathenaic prize amphora). Identifying it as 'a Greek vase / pottery' is partial; the black-figure Attic amphora identification is the core.
scoring_anchors.primary_source_rule: canonical = the Met's own catalog record (Met page or Met Collection API). Wikipedia/stock/auction reproductions are secondary.
scoring_anchors.tolerance: Accept 'Panathenaic amphora' / 'Attic black-figure amphora' for the object type. Full credit requires reaching the Met record (object id 247960 or accession 07.286.80). If the executor correctly types it as an Attic black-figure Panathenaic amphora but cannot pin the exact Met object id, give partial credit; pinning the Met record is required for full credit since the pixels are from the Met image.
